name: Mount Candina and Ojos del Diablo Loop
name_original: Solpico - Ojo del Diablo por la Playa de San Julián
description: Hike the difficult Solpico - Ojo del Diablo via San Julian Beach trail, featuring panoramic views, a secluded beach, and a natural rock wind
region_intro: Hiking around Liendo offers diverse natural landscapes, blending coastal and mountainous scenery. The region features unique geological formations like rock arches and varied terrain, from accessible paths to more challenging routes with carved rock steps. Hikers can explore picturesque beaches and extensive views over the Liendo Valley and the Cantabrian coast. This area provides a range of experiences for different fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Liendo The most popular hiking route is Mount Candina and Ojos del Diablo Loop, a 7.6 miles (12.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 24 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers expansive views of the coast and features the notable Devil's Eye rock arches. Another top favourite among local hikers is Devil's Eye – Devil's Eyes Rock Arches loop from Cueto Gedo, a moderate 4.0 miles (6.4 km) path. This trail showcases distinctive geological formations and offers views of the surrounding landscape. Local hikers also love the Tree Growing from Rock – View of La Peña mountain loop from Restaurante Hospedaje Valle de Liendo, a 10.6 miles (17.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views of La Peña mountain, often completed in about 6 hours 18 minutes. Hiking around Liendo is defined by diverse coastal paths, mountainous backdrops, and unique geological formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Liendo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 600 reviews. Nearly 4,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Liendo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find in Liendo?
A: Liendo offers a diverse range of hiking trails, blending captivating coastal views with mountainous scenery. You'll find paths suitable for various fitness levels, from easily accessible routes to more challenging ascents featuring unique geological formations like rock arches and varied terrain.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes in Liendo suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Liendo has several accessible paths perfect for beginners or families. While specific easy routes are not detailed here, the region offers 2 easy trails among its 21 routes, ensuring options for those seeking a less strenuous experience.

Q: Where can I find unique natural features or landmarks while hiking in Liendo?
A: Liendo is known for its distinctive natural features. You can explore the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1834895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Devil's Eye&lt;/a&gt; rock arches, which are part of routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996594600/mount-candina-and-ojos-del-diablo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mount Candina and Ojos del Diablo Loop&lt;/a&gt;. Another intriguing sight is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4416895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lone Tree on the Way to Ojos del Diablo&lt;/a&gt;, often found on trails that offer views of La Peña mountain.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Liendo?
A: Yes, many of Liendo's trails are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31879837" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Devil's Eye – Devil's Eyes Rock Arches loop from Cueto Gedo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.4 km circular path showcasing distinctive geological formations.

Q: What are the best trails for experiencing coastal and mountain views in Liendo?
A: For expansive coastal and mountain views, consider routes that traverse the diverse landscapes of Liendo. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996594600/mount-candina-and-ojos-del-diablo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mount Candina and Ojos del Diablo Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers breathtaking vistas over the beach and town of Sonabia, extending to the Biscayan coast and the Liendo Valley.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Liendo?
A: The hiking trails in Liendo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 600 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse natural landscapes, the captivating blend of coastal and mountainous scenery, and the unique geological formations encountered along the routes.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to beaches or offer views of them?
A: Yes, Liendo's natural appeal includes its beautiful beaches. Some hiking loops, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20506861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa Sonabia – Tree Growing from Rock loop from Parque de Sonabia&lt;/a&gt;, incorporate or offer views of beaches like Playa Sonabia, providing picturesque coastal scenery.

Q: What is the most challenging hike in Liendo?
A: For a more challenging experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38189588" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tree Growing from Rock – View of La Peña mountain loop from Restaurante Hospedaje Valle de Liendo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 17.1 km trail. It leads through varied terrain with significant elevation changes and offers rewarding views of La Peña mountain.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or summits accessible via hiking trails in Liendo?
A: Absolutely. Liendo offers several viewpoints and summits. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4486690" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Solpico Summit&lt;/a&gt; is a notable peak, and you can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5389027" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marshes Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which provides scenic vistas of the surrounding landscape.

Q: How many hiking routes are available in Liendo?
A: There are over 20 hiking routes available in Liendo, offering a wide variety of options for different preferences and skill levels. This includes 2 easy, 13 moderate, and 6 difficult trails.

Q: Can I find trails that combine geological formations with other natural sights?
A: Yes, many trails in Liendo combine its unique geological formations with other natural beauty. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10509058" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Devil's Eye – Tree Growing from Rock loop from Playa de San Julián&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.4 km route that allows you to experience both the distinctive rock arches and the famous 'Tree Growing from Rock' feature.
tips: Beautiful secluded beach just 15 minutes from Camino del Norte
At present, only ruins remain, but since the 9th century this hermitage has been visited by pilgrims traveling the Camino de Santiago del Norte.
Summit overlooking the Ojos del Diablo and Laredo

One of the most visited sites in Cantabria, this viewpoint offers majestic panoramic views from the cliff framed by a natural rock window that are worthy of a postcard.
Getting here has its challenging part, but you will enjoy a spectacular and picturesque route with impressive views.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Solpico - Ojo del Diablo via San Julian Beach hike?
faqs.answer: This route is generally considered difficult, requiring good physical fitness, sure-footedness, and sturdy footwear. While some sources might suggest an easier classification, the varied terrain and elevation gain mean it's best approached with preparation. It covers 12.3 km with an elevation gain of 517 meters.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features a mix of designated hiking paths, mountain hiking paths, and some street sections. You'll encounter varied surfaces, so sturdy footwear is essential for navigating the different terrains.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll discover several notable features. Approximately 2.72 km into the route, you'll reach Playa de San Julián, a beautiful secluded beach. Further along, you can explore the ruins of the Hermitage of San Julián. The Solpico Summit offers commanding views, and the Devil's Eye (Ojo del Diablo) provides spectacular panoramic views through a natural rock window.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its classification as a difficult hike, requiring good physical fitness and sure-footedness, this route is generally not recommended for beginners or young children. It's best suited for experienced hikers.

faqs.question: Is the Solpico - Ojo del Diablo via San Julian Beach trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding specific dog regulations for this exact trail is not readily available. However, as a general rule for challenging hikes in natural areas, dogs should be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure their safety on varied terrain. Always check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: While specific seasonal conditions are not detailed, generally, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant hiking weather in Cantabria, with milder temperatures and less chance of extreme heat or cold. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that a permit or entrance fee is required to access the Solpico - Ojo del Diablo via San Julian Beach trail.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details for the trailhead are not provided in the available information. However, common access points for trails in this region often include parking areas near towns like Oriñón or Laredo. It's advisable to research parking options near the starting point before your visit, potentially looking for parking at N-634 km 161 as suggested by user queries.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Senda del Litoral, Ruta cima Solpico y Candina, Ruta a los Arcos de Llanegro (Ojos del Diablo), and the Plano inclinado minero.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty, you should bring sturdy hiking boots, sufficient water, snacks, appropriate clothing layers for changing weather, sun protection, and a map or GPS device. Trekking poles can also be beneficial for stability on varied terrain.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: While there are no specific establishments mentioned directly on the trail, you can typically find cafes and restaurants in nearby towns such as Laredo or Oriñón, which are common starting or ending points for hikes in the region.
